The Secret Life of Pets (2016)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $369,094,830Details
International Box Office $516,918,170
Worldwide Box Office $886,013,000
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $50,158,988
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $51,307,008
Total $101,465,996
Further financial details...

Synopsis

For one bustling Manhattan apartment building, the real day starts after the folks on two legs leave for work and school. That’s when the pets of every stripe, fur and feather begin their own nine-to-five routine: hanging out with each other, trading humiliating stories about their owners, auditioning adorable looks to get better snacks and watching Animal Planet like it is reality TV.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$104,352,905 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 11.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,370 opening theaters/4,381 max. theaters, 8.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $482,283,633

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 8th, 2016 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: May 8th, 2016 (Wide) (Sweden)
June 24th, 2016 (Wide) (Ireland)
June 24th, 2016 (Wide) (Norway)
June 24th, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 1st, 2016 (Wide) (Taiwan)
Video Release: November 22nd, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
November 14th, 2016 by Universal Pictures UK
MPA Rating: PG for action and some rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2416 (Cert #50344), 3/16/2016)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: The Secret Life of Pets
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Paris, France
Filming location (country): France
Filming location (production facility): Illumination Mac Guff Studios, Paris, France
Geographical setting: Brooklyn, New York, New York City
Plot point: Missing Pet, Rescue, Revenge, Talking Animals
Social setting: Slobs vs. Snobs
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Illumination Entertainment, Universal Pictures, Chris Meledandri, Dentsu Inc., Fuji Television Network
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

For one bustling Manhattan apartment building, the real day starts after the folks on two legs leave for work and school. That’s when the pets of every stripe, fur and feather begin their own nine-to-five routine: hanging out with each other, trading humiliating stories about their owners, auditioning adorable looks to get better snacks and watching Animal Planet like it is reality TV.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$104,352,905 (28.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.54 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:41.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$75,000,000 (worldwide box office is 11.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,370 opening theaters/4,381 max. theaters, 8.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $482,283,633

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 8th, 2016 (Wide) by Universal
International Releases: May 8th, 2016 (Wide) (Sweden)

June 24th, 2016 (Wide) (Norway)
June 24th, 2016 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
July 1st, 2016 (Wide) (Taiwan)
Video Release: November 22nd, 2016 by Universal Home Entertainment
November 14th, 2016 by Universal Pictures UK
MPA Rating: PG for action and some rude humor.
(Rating bulletin 2416 (Cert #50344), 3/16/2016)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Franchise: The Secret Life of Pets
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D, IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Paris, France

Plot point: Missing Pet, Rescue, Revenge, Talking Animals
Social setting: Slobs vs. Snobs
Subgenre: Family Adventure

Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English
